title: Chhattisgarh braces for harsher heat as 'Nautapa' begins today: Raipur hits 45°C; heatwave likely in centra... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Chhattisgarh braces for harsher heat as 'Nautapa' begins today:Raipur hits 45°C; heatwave likely in central districts for next 4 days
With the beginning of Nautapa on Monday, heat conditions across Chhattisgarh are expected to intensify further. The Meteorological Department has forecast heatwave-like conditions in several parts of central Chhattisgarh over the next four days, while the capital city Raipur has already recorded temperatures touching 45 degrees Celsius.
Even during the night, warm winds continue to sweep through many areas, offering little relief from the scorching weather.
At present, weather conditions across the state remain completely dry, with no significant drop in temperature recorded over the past 24 hours.
The highest maximum temperature in the state was recorded at 45.5 degrees Celsius in Rajnandgaon, while the lowest minimum temperature stood at 24 degrees Celsius in Ambikapur.
Nautapa considered the hottest phase of summer
Nautapa, which began on May 25, is considered the hottest period of the year in both astrological and weather traditions. During these nine days, the sun’s rays fall almost directly on the earth, causing temperatures to rise sharply.
According to the Meteorological Department, more than 50 cities in India are currently among the hottest locations globally. Experts believe temperatures could climb further during Nautapa, increasing health risks linked to extreme heat.
Doctors have advised people to make small changes to their daily routine and diet during this period, warning that excessive heat can place additional stress on the body, including the heart and brain.
Hot winds to continue across central Chhattisgarh
The weather department said intense sunshine and hot winds are likely to persist in several districts, including Raipur, Durg, Rajnandgaon, Balodabazar and Mahasamund.
Meteorological scientists said there is little possibility of any major fall in temperatures over the next four days. A slight drop of 1 to 3 degrees Celsius may occur after that, but dry and hot weather is expected to continue for now.
The local forecast for Raipur predicts mainly clear skies on May 25, with maximum temperatures likely to remain around 45 degrees Celsius and minimum temperatures near 28 degrees Celsius.
Around 200 bats die from suspected heatstroke in Korba
The impact of the severe heatwave has also been seen on wildlife. Around 200 bats reportedly died due to heatstroke in Korba district.
According to forest officials, hundreds of bats fell from trees near Naukonia pond in the Pali development block on Saturday. Pali Forest Range Ranger Yogeshwar Banjare confirmed that the deaths were linked to the intense heatwave conditions in the area, where temperatures have remained between 42 and 43 degrees Celsius.
Thousands of migratory bats usually gather around the Naukonia pond region during this period.
Health advisory issued as Bilaspur records 44°C
Following temperatures touching 44 degrees Celsius in Bilaspur, the Health Department has issued an advisory asking people to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours.
Residents have been advised to stay hydrated, drink plenty of water, wear light cotton clothes and avoid direct exposure to sunlight between 12 noon and 4 pm.
Children, elderly people and pregnant women have been identified as the most vulnerable groups during the ongoing heatwave.
Emergency helpline numbers issued by the department are 87706-64365 and 75870-38622.
